title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

title: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
Rubio hails Lebanon for peace efforts after meeting president
subhead: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio commended the Lebanese government for its 'determined effort to reclaim Lebanon's sovereignty, disarm Hezbollah and dismantle its terrorist infrastructure, and move towards peace.'

title: Gaza's children, traumatized and mutilated by war: 'His prosthesis hurts, but he endures it to show that he isn't different'
subhead: Due to the bombings carried out by Israel since October 7, 2023, the Gaza Strip now holds the grim record for the highest number of amputee children per capita in the world, according to the World Health Organization. These children have been trying to rebuild their lives despite forced displacement and lack of medical care in a devastated territory.

title: The forest's slow return after fires: 'It feels like all life is gone, but in reality, much remains'
subhead: The ecological consequences of wildfires are difficult to quantify and vary due to many factors. What is certain is that the intensity and frequency of fires can be especially damaging to ecosystems.

title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: Why Russia's hybrid warfare is forcing France to raise its voice
subhead: Between acts of sabotage and drone flyovers, the General Directorate of Internal Security (DGSI) attributed a large-scale cyberespionage campaign to Russian domestic intelligence. In response, the chargé d'affaires at the Russian embassy in Paris was summoned by the foreign minister on Friday, July 17.

title: After three heatwaves, French people worry: 'Next year will be worse. How will we cope?'
subhead: Across France, people of all ages have realized how vulnerable they are to extreme temperatures, which have stunned the country during these past weeks.
